name: SC SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: South Carolina's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the SC Department of Social Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Healthy Connections (SC Medicaid)
description: South Carolina's Medicaid program providing free or low-cost health coverage for eligible residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, and preventive services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Children up to 213% FPL; pregnant women up to 199% FPL; adults vary by category

name: SC Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the SC Department of Employment and Workforce.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: SC Housing Finance and Development Authority
description: Provides affordable housing resources including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, and housing counseling for South Carolina residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI

name: SC Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income South Carolinians in matters including housing, family law, consumer issues, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below; some programs may have different limits

name: SC Works (Workforce Development)
description: South Carolina's workforce development system offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling through local SC Works centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All South Carolina residents seeking employment or career development services

name: SC LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay their heating and cooling bills. Administered through the SC Office of Economic Opportunity.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly and disabled
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: SC 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting South Carolinians with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All South Carolina residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Harvest Hope Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 20 counties in central South Carolina. Distributes food through a network of partner agencies, pantries, and mobile distributions.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the service area

name: Lowcountry Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 10 coastal counties in South Carolina. Provides food distribution, nutrition education, and community programs to combat hunger.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Lowcountry region

name: Feeding the Carolinas - Golden Harvest Food Bank
description: Feeding America member food bank serving the Upstate and Midlands regions of South Carolina through partner agencies and food distribution programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Upstate or Midlands area

name: SC Department of Veterans' Affairs
description: Assists South Carolina veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and burial benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in SC

name: SC Crisis Line (SCDMH)
description: South Carolina Department of Mental Health crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in South Carolina experiencing a mental health crisis

name: SC Voucher (ABC Child Care Program)
description: South Carolina's childcare assistance program helping low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work or attend school. Administered by SC DSS.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children who need childcare to work or attend school/training
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 150% FPL initially; up to 85% SMI to maintain

name: SC TANF (Family Independence Program)
description: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families providing cash assistance and supportive services to low-income families with children while helping parents achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children who meet work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: SC Lottery Tuition Assistance Program
description: Provides tuition assistance for South Carolina residents attending in-state two-year public or independent institutions. Funded by the SC Education Lottery.
eligibilitySummary: SC residents enrolled at eligible two-year institutions taking at least 6 credit hours

name: SC LIFE Scholarship
description: Merit-based scholarship for South Carolina residents attending eligible in-state institutions. Covers tuition costs and provides a book stipend.
eligibilitySummary: SC residents meeting GPA, SAT/ACT, and class rank requirements entering eligible SC colleges

name: SC Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs and improve comfort and safety. Includes insulation, air sealing, and HVAC repairs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority given to elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: CARTA (Charleston Area Regional Transportation Authority)
description: Public transit system serving the Charleston metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit. Offers reduced fares for eligible riders.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Charleston metro area; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and Medicare cardholders
hours: Varies by route; generally Monday-Saturday 5:30 AM - 10:30 PM

name: The Comet (Central Midlands Regional Transit Authority)
description: Public transit system serving the Columbia metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options for the Midlands region.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Columbia metro area

name: SC Bar Pro Bono Program
description: Connects low-income individuals with volunteer attorneys who provide free legal assistance in civil matters including family law, housing, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals who cannot afford legal representation

name: SC Thrive
description: Benefits screening and enrollment service helping South Carolinians identify and apply for public assistance programs. Free benefits check available online and through community partners.
eligibilitySummary: All South Carolina residents seeking to identify available benefits

name: Greenville Free Medical Clinic
description: Provides free medical, dental, and pharmaceutical services to uninsured, low-income adults in the Greenville area who do not qualify for other assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured adults in the Greenville area at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 200% FPL or below; must be uninsured

name: Trident United Way (Charleston)
description: United Way serving the Charleston tri-county area providing programs in education, financial stability, and health. Operates a 211 helpline and funds local partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Berkeley, Charleston, and Dorchester counties

name: SC WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5

name: SC Emergency Rental Assistance Program
description: Provides financial assistance to renters and landlords for past-due rent and utility payments to prevent eviction and housing instability.
eligibilitySummary: Renters who have experienced financial hardship and are at risk of homelessness or housing instability
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% AMI

name: SC Department of Aging - Senior Services
description: Provides services for seniors including home-delivered meals, transportation, caregiver support, legal assistance, and health insurance counseling through the aging network.
eligibilitySummary: South Carolina residents age 60 and older and their caregivers

name: Palmetto Community Action Partnership
description: Community action agency providing comprehensive anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, financial literacy, housing counseling, and workforce development.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Midlands region

name: SC Vocational Rehabilitation Department
description: Helps South Carolinians with disabilities prepare for, achieve, and maintain competitive employment through training, counseling, and support services.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: SC Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and referrals to local shelters for victims of domestic violence and their families.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in South Carolina
languages: English, Spanish, and 200+ languages via interpreter

name: Head Start / Early Head Start SC
description: Federally funded early childhood education program promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income families. Includes comprehensive health and family services.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines; foster children and homeless children automatically eligible
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; certain categorical eligibility applies

name: Columbia Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Columbia area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Columbia area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Greenville County Human Relations Commission
description: Investigates discrimination complaints and promotes equal opportunity in housing, employment, and public accommodations in Greenville County.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Greenville County who has experienced discrimination

name: SC Healthy Connections CHIP
description: Children's Health Insurance Program providing affordable health coverage for uninsured children in families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income above Medicaid limits but below CHIP thresholds
incomeLimitNotes: Children in families up to 213% FPL

name: SC Telephone Assistance Program (Lifeline)
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income South Carolina households to ensure access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program
